response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial domain interest through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated distributed-systems thinking from his Dunnhumby tenure handling high-throughput traffic. However, the role is fundamentally a C++/Rust systems engineering position focused on routing tile pipelines, and his C++ exposure is limited to brief early-career work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — both over a decade ago and neither at the scale or complexity the role demands. His primary production stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, with no Rust and no evidence of modern C++ in commercial settings. Routing tile systems, navigation guidance pipelines, and the latency-sensitive data-freshness challenges described in the JD are entirely absent from his history.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain exposure via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application, including a FOSS4G 2019 talk
Demonstrated distributed-systems thinking at Dunnhumby: async I/O, low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Consistent and principled QA discipline (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development) across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and stale — limited to NI LabWindows work in 2014–2015 and a university thesis; no Rust mentioned anywhere
Primary production stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which is misaligned with the C++ systems programming core of this role
Zero experience with routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or map-data freshness/latency optimization — the central responsibilities of the position
